name: Chocolate Rolled Fondant
recipeIngredient: 6 1/4 cups powdered sugar (lightly spooned into cup) 1 pound 9 ounces
2 cups unsweetened cocoa (Dutch Processed) or 2 1/2 cups nonalkalized cocoa such as Hershey's
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle gelatin over the water in a 2-cup heatproof glass measure or bowl and let sit for 5 minutes.
2. Place in a small pan of simmering water or microwave on high power, stirring once or twice until dissolved.
3. Blend in corn syrup and glycerine, then add shortening and stir until melted. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla.
4. Mix sugar and cocoa in a large bowl and make a well in the center.
5. Add gelatin mixture and stir with a wooden spoon until blended.
6. Mix with hands and knead vigorously until a ball forms.
7. Turn out onto a smooth, lightly greased surface such as Formica or marble, clean hands, and knead until smooth and satiny.
8. If the fondant is dry or brittle, add several drops of water and knead well.
9. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and place in an airtight container.
10. When ready to roll out, spray work surface and rolling pin with nonstick vegetable spray.
11. Roll out until smooth and satiny; tiny cracks are normal due to warmth or pressure.
12. If cocoa is lumpy, process briefly in food processor until powdery.
13. Store fondant covered at room temperature for up to 2 days.
Note: Do not substitute butter for solid white vegetable shortening, as it blends better and does not affect flavor.
